CONTINUED FROM ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE CALENDAR ON FEB-06-2025, TO ASBESTOS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE CALENDAR ON FEB-05-2026, AT 9:00 AM IN DEPT. 304 PER REQUEST OF COUNSEL. THE MATTER IS CONTINUED DUE TO A FEDERAL STAY IN BANKRUPTCY COURT. COUNSEL IS TO ADVISE THE COURT OF ANY MOVEMENT AS TO INGERSOLL-RAND COMPANY. DISCUSSIONS ARE HELD AS TO 524G AND THE TEXAS TWO-STEP CONTROVERSY. MS. LETSCH STATES THAT THEY ARE FIGHTING FOR A RETURN OF THESE MATTERS TO THE TORT SYSTEM, SO THESE CASES ARE LEFT ON THE COURT'S CALENDAR. MS. CHU STATES IF THEY DISMISS THAT THERE ARE CONCERNS AS TO WHETHER THEY WILL BE ABLE TO COME BACK AND THAT THERE WILL BE A GREAT PREJUDICE TO THEIR CLIENTS. THE COURT STATES THAT GOING FORWARD IT WOULD LIKE PLAINTIFF TO SUBMIT ONE LETTER TO THE COURT AT LEAST EVERY SIX MONTHS. THE LETTER IS TO STATE IF A PLAN HAS BEEN PROPOSED, IS TO PROVIDE WHAT THE TIMING OF THE PLAN WILL BE, AND IS TO INCLUDE AN UPDATE ON THE CURRENT STATUS OF THE BANKRUPTCY. KIMBERLY CHU, ESQ., AND BRYN LETSCH, ESQ., OF BRAYTON PURCELL APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF VIA ZOOM. JUDGE: ETHAN P. SCHULMAN, CLERK: J. ALAMEDA, NOT REPORTED. (304)

ASBESTOS CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jun-04-2020 Set For Trial On Sep-28-20 At 11:15 A.M. In Dept 503

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff
Off Calendar Jun-04-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kimberly J. Chu of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing through CourtCall for plaintiff Lyle Olsen.
Emily Anselmo of WFBM, LLP (415-781-7072), appearing through CourtCall for defendant CBS Corporation and General Electric Company.
Jon C. James of HUGO & PARKER (415-808-0300), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Foster Wheeler.
Fred Gatt of LEADER, BERKON, COLAO & SILVERSTEIN LLP (213-234-1750), appearing through CourtCall for defendant Imo Industries.

RULING: PLAINTIFF'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR ORDER GRANTING PREFERENCE IN SETTING CASE FOR TRIAL, AND EXTENDING DISCOVERY CUTOFF

Hearing held, argued and tentative ruling adopted: Plaintiffs' motion for preference is granted pursuant to C.C.P. 36(a). No opposition filed. The trial date is September 28, 2020 at 11:15 a.m. in Department 503. Last day pursuant to C.C.P. 36(f) is October 2, 2020. Local Rule 20 shall be followed by the parties as well as all San Francisco Local Rules and California Rules of Court. The last day for hearing summary judgment/adjudication motions is September 24, 2020. Summary judgment/adjudication motions shall be brought on regular notice pursuant to the relevant provisions of the C.C.P., unless the parties stipulate otherwise. The Court will allow a maximum of four summary judgment/adjudication motions per day to be calendared in this action. Before a party files and serves such motion, it must contact the Court's Calendar Clerk to make a reservation. Time to respond to written discovery that has not yet been served is shortened to 20 days. For written discovery that has already been served, responses are due within 20 days of this hearing or by the date determined by the C.C.P., whichever is earlier. If there is any kind of issue/dispute that requires meet and confer, such meet and confer shall not be made via email or letter. It shall occur in person or via telephone. Electronic service is considered the equivalent of personal service. Fact discovery cut-off date is September 11, 2020. Expert discovery cut-off date is September 25, 2020. All bankruptcy documents shall be turned over to defendants no later than July 3, 2020. If plaintiffs submit documents to bankruptcy trusts after this date, they must notify defendants of this no later than five days after submission. The fact discovery cut-off does not apply to bankruptcy documents.
(Mini Minutes continued, see next entry)

Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Granting Preference In Setting Case For Trial, And Extending Discovery Cutoff (Added To Calendar For Issuance Of Tentative Ruling Only)
Off Calendar Jun-04-2020 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: (PART 2 OF 2)
(Mini Minutes continued from previous entry)

No later than June 18, 2020 plaintiffs must provide a statement to each defendant stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of plaintiffs' claims as to each defendant. No later than June 18, 2020 defendants must provide a statement stating the name and contact information of each lay person with knowledge of any facts supporting any of defendant's affirmative defenses. Any person not identified on the statements may be barred from testifying at trial or presenting a declaration on a summary motion absent good cause shown. Further case management conference on August 5, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. No need for any party to file any case management conference statement. Parties may appear at the conference by telephone. If the parties agree that no case management conference is necessary, they shall notify the Court at least two day prior to take the CMC off calendar. Plaintiffs to make a settlement demand on defendants no later than August 14, 2020. Responses to the demand are due no later than August 28, 2020. The parties are ordered to provide confidential settlement demands/offers stating their best and final settlement effort. This confidential value must be separately submitted to Commissioner Pang Ly via electronic mail by September 18, 2020 at 12:00 p.m. Mandatory settlement conference with only the attorneys present and final decision makers available by phone is set for Thursday, September 24, 2020 at 1:30 p.m. in 603. Order signed in open court. Judge: Cynthia Ming-mei Lee, Clerk: A. Mok. Not reported. (503/CML)
